seo_description: Current time in Kampala, Uganda. East Africa Time (EAT, UTC+3) year-round — no DST. Live clock and time differences for Kampala.

faq.question: What time is it in Kampala right now?
faq.answer: Kampala, Uganda uses East Africa Time (EAT, UTC+3) year-round. Uganda does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so clocks never change. The IANA identifier is Africa/Kampala. Kampala shares the UTC+3 offset with Nairobi (Kenya), Dar es Salaam (Tanzania), Addis Ababa (Ethiopia), and the entire East African Community region. The live clock above shows the current Kampala time.

faq.question: What time zone is Kampala in?
faq.answer: Kampala, Uganda uses East Africa Time (EAT, UTC+3) year-round. The IANA identifier is Africa/Kampala. Uganda does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Kampala is located almost exactly on the equator, meaning daylight hours barely vary throughout the year — one reason DST is not needed.

faq.question: Does Kampala observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Kampala, Uganda does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Uganda uses East Africa Time (EAT, UTC+3) permanently. Countries near the equator, including Uganda, have very little seasonal variation in daylight hours, making DST unnecessary.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Kampala and London?
faq.answer: Kampala (EAT, UTC+3) is 3 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer time (BST, UTC+1), Kampala is 2 hours ahead. Uganda does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Kampala and New York?
faq.answer: Kampala (EAT, UTC+3) is 8 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Kampala is 7 hours ahead. Uganda does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Kampala?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Kampala are 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM EAT, Monday through Friday. Kampala is Uganda's capital and largest city, and East Africa's third-largest city. Major industries include telecommunications, banking, and trade. The Uganda Securities Exchange (USE) is located in Kampala. Uganda has one of Africa's youngest and fastest-growing populations, driving demand in consumer goods, mobile banking, and technology sectors.
